condensed.new_desc: Daemon startup arg is one pre-generated rkyv Configure message (deploy helper/CLI authors+encodes NOTA); bootstrap needs no manager. Daemon opens the named store: virgin -> apply Configure as first config; populated -> self-resume. Same Configure type accepted live over the meta socket for runtime reconfig, so bootstrap and runtime config are one vocabulary; Configure is the baseline meta op of every meta-signal-component contract.
condensed.reason: Kept dual-mode store, one-vocabulary Configure, baseline-meta-op arrow; dropped binary-only/never-flags restatement (a hard-override) and prose framing. Referent meta-signal->meta-signal-component (more specific contract leg), added nota.

condensed.new_desc: Each component engine defines Signal, Nexus, SEMA interfaces in schema and runs core logic via schema-emitted traits over root types. Strict ownership: SEMA owns durable state/storage, Nexus owns decision-making/request-execution (Nexus is the execution engine, renamed from executor), Signal owns communication. Flow: Signal in -> Nexus -> SEMA (on state work) -> Nexus -> Signal -> client. Engine-role names are traits, not one component's enum. This runtime LOGIC triad differs from the repo PACKAGING triad (component + signal-* + meta-signal-*).
condensed.reason: Restored the executor->Nexus rename arrow (a distinct naming fact). Kept three ownerships, flow order, traits-not-enum, logic-vs-packaging-triad distinction; cut handwritten-runtime terseness narration.

condensed.new_desc: Secrets land in the most secure store available: gopass (git-backed, encrypted) in-session; SOPS-nix (decrypted only on target host at activation) for cluster deploy. Move ciphertext-to-ciphertext, preferring blind pipe-to-sink, never via a visible intermediate. Inspect plaintext only for a psyche/task-authorized job; keep it out of chat, reports, logs, commits, argv, traces, Nix store, fixtures, checked-in files. Local LLM API token installs via a command-backed gopass reference, never a literal key in Nix/models.json.
condensed.reason: Restored blind-pipe-to-sink preference arrow. Kept both stores, ciphertext-only movement, exposure surfaces, gopass-reference rule; dropped 'workspace skills must state this' meta.

condensed.new_desc: Treat the language's structural-macro set as data, not a frozen compiler set: every construct (struct/enum/fn/impl/generics) is a value of a schema type, so the language is infinitely extensible and LLM-readable with no privileged escape-hatch. A file is a typed tree of structural-form nodes; the grammar is the type, the type is the reader, so no parser is hand-written. NOTA's own grammar is schema-expressible (self-hosting); its delimiter set is a closed enum (three base pairs + three piped, two reserved). Next: build the schema spec for the macro node.
condensed.reason: Kept macro-as-data, grammar-is-type-no-parser, self-hosting + closed delimiter enum, and the next-step; trimmed the conceptual essay on conventional languages.

condensed.new_desc: Sema-engine is the exclusive DB boundary: all durable state goes through it (writes to the .sema DB file, currently redb); components are storage-dumb and speak only sema-engine ops, no direct redb calls. SEMA is a single-writer actor; reads run parallel over redb MVCC. For lojix cutover build durable DB backing first (live-generation-set, GC-roots, event-log persisted with self-resume), not in-memory first. When schema-derived components expose a reusable storage need, improve the shared engine surface rather than port to a mismatched API. The schema component holds the compiled binary runtime schema (O(1) lookup, version-diff/namespace tables); NOTA is the source authoring format. Raw mirror/handover payloads stay outside the typed DB.
condensed.reason: Restored the improve-shared-engine-surface arrow (distinct: grow shared surface vs port to mismatched API). Kept exclusive-boundary, single-writer/MVCC, .sema naming, durable-first cutover, schema-vs-NOTA split, raw-payload separation.

condensed.new_desc: Keep working productively: dispatch subagents non-blocking and do other work while in flight, integrating results at a natural point with multiple tools in flight. Mid-task info the psyche injects is context to absorb, not a stop; only an explicit stop interrupts. Treat transient connection warnings as non-blocking while tooling works. Work the bead/code path immediately; beads are coordination aids, not a substitute for implementation. In troubleshooting where a working result was asked, apply a narrow safe fix, not diagnosis-only. After subagent prototype work, run context maintenance + fresh-intent audit, then synthesize and implement immediate fixes.
condensed.reason: Kept all six arrows (non-blocking dispatch, inject-isn't-stop, transient-warning, bead path, narrow-fix, post-prototype audit); reframed from prohibitions to affirmative and dropped war-story phrasing. Verified complete and affirmative.

condensed.new_desc: NOTA extension is type-directed structural matching over raw-parser nodes (preserving delimiters, counts, sigils, nesting). The structural macro node is a NOTA enum decoded by shape: codec matches variants in declaration order (most-specific first, first wins), recursively; encode emits the matching block (bidirectional). Realize via #[derive(StructuralMacroNode)] with per-variant shape attributes, not a runtime registry or string-name dispatch. Macros are typed NOTA records (name, structural-match pattern, capture specs, position), dispatched as an ordered list with conflict detection at registry construction. Pattern language is a typed enum of primitives (arity, atom-case, atom-sigil, delimiter-type, atom literal) so macro data stays serializable.
condensed.reason: All arrows preserved (type-directed structural matching over raw-parser nodes, declaration-order/first-wins recursive codec, bidirectional encode, derive realization vs runtime/string dispatch, ordered dispatch+conflict detection at registry construction, typed serializable pattern primitives); only restatement trimmed. Referents are kebab-case particulars.

condensed.new_desc: All Rust production functions are methods on non-zero-sized data-bearing structs/enums, or trait methods (preferred). Forbidden: free functions, impls on zero-sized placeholder/marker structs used as namespaces (a free function in disguise), and module-level const-fn and async-fn. Exempt: functions under cfg-test and fn main. The rule forces four decisions per author: what objects exist, where data lives, where logic lives, what methods belong on what. Carry it strongly in the rust skill.
condensed.reason: Kept the rule, ZST-marker ban, const/async-fn module-level ban, cfg-test+main exemptions, four-decisions rationale, carry-in-rust-skill; dropped 'revise if stale / rewrite violators' war-story. Referents kebab-case particulars (cfg-test added, accurate).

condensed.new_desc: Guardian does semantic judgment on every guarded write; deterministic code gathers context, validates structure, applies typed verdict consequences. It judges a submission across justification-read dimensions (justifies intent, correct domain, certainty/importance fit) and judges a cross-record operation whole as one yes/no. Supersede atomically replaces a retired set with multiple new intents; guardian checks the new set together preserves the kernel. Each op type gets a per-op prompt with worked accept/reject examples (over-train then scale back), and always returns well-formed NOTA via generated verdict grammar, closed rejection set, parse-and-retry, shape test. A clean-context specialized guardian beats the submitting agent even on a weaker model.
condensed.reason: All arrows preserved (judgment dimensions, whole-op single verdict, atomic supersede kernel check, per-op prompts with over-train, NOTA-validity machinery, specialization-beats-strength). Referents kebab-case particulars.

condensed.new_desc: Schema files live with their contract's source-of-truth; a repo may hold multiple .schema files (one per contract), named <crate>/<contract-name>.schema. Wire/signal schemas live in signal-<component> as the cross-component compilation boundary Rust sees as one canonical source; stable public signal schemas sit in contract repos separate from daemon logic so consumers aren't rebuilt on internal changes. Storage-contract and internal actor-message schemas live with the daemon (daemon+database+internal actors are one process/compilation unit). A daemon's configuration type lives in the signal contract (imported for binary startup decode; meta-signal Configure wraps it), not hand-written in the daemon. Signal repos carry only signal/wire vocabulary; runtime-plane, storage, provider-effect, and REST schema belong in the runtime component.
condensed.reason: Every location arrow preserved (multi-schema naming, wire in signal-component as compilation boundary, stable contracts separate, storage+actor with daemon, config type in signal contract via meta-signal Configure, signal-repos-wire-only). Referents kebab-case (meta-signal added, accurate).

condensed.new_desc: Foundational schema model (supersedes the at-sigil for macro invocation): EVERYTHING is a struct — unit variant = zero-field struct, data variant = one-field struct, enum = single-field struct holding the active variant. A schema node is a tagged variant (paren: first object = tag picking the variant of the known expected type, second = carried data), read against the schema-of-schemas, so positional typing resolves variant-vs-macro with no sigil. A macro is a Rust struct; macro-space is a tagged union of them; invoking is a tagged variant against the known macro type. The schema is one recursive shape: a root struct with macro-expanded fields down to scalar leaves; macros expand structurally by position; a delimiter shape (e.g. bracket field vector) means struct/newtype only at the lowerer's position; recursion terminates at built-in macros over scalar leaves (ints, unit-variant enums, bools, typed-newtype strings).
condensed.reason: All arrows preserved (everything-is-struct decomposition, positional tagged-variant typing against schema-of-schemas resolving variant-vs-macro sigil-free, macro-as-struct/macro-space-tagged-union, single recursive shape with positional expansion and scalar-leaf termination). Restored 'only at the lowerer's position'. Referents kebab-case particulars.

condensed.new_desc: A contract is a channel; each channel gets its own schema (wire/repo contract, storage contract, each internal-actor-message vocabulary), so one component has multiple .schema files, one per channel — superseding one-schema-per-component. A component is at least three plane schemas: the Signal wire contract lives wire-only in the contract repo; Nexus and SEMA plane schemas are separate .schema files inside the daemon crate (e.g. cloud/schema/nexus.schema), each with own imports/exports/namespace and importing the wire Signal IO. Plane schemas are not separate crates/repos. Schemas warrant from need-to-interact: every part worth describing gets a schema the psyche can inspect and interact with, and signal-frame itself is schema.
condensed.reason: Channel=schema, multi-schema-per-component, three-plane placement (wire in contract repo; Nexus/SEMA in daemon crate), not-separate-crates, schema-of-everything-from-need-to-interact, signal-frame-is-schema all preserved; dropped 'load-bearing/embedded across ESSENCE/AGENTS/...' meta-commentary. Removed vague 'spirit' referent (not a named particular in this essay); kept named particulars.

condensed.new_desc: In schema-next, a bare @-sigil type at a field/variant position derives the binding name from the type: @Topics ≡ topics@Topics (field=camelCase of type), @(Vec Topic) from the composite, @SomethingHoldingSomething a data variant named after the held type. Bare PascalCase at an enum position = unit variant; explicit name@Type still works. EnumVariants macro supports compact self-tagged variants (one-element paren = tag-and-type; paren name with brace body = named struct, bracket body = named enum); inline enum-body names resolve unit vs data against the type table (a type ⇒ data-carrying). Assembled model unchanged: Vec<(Name, TypeReference)> for fields, named Variant for enum variants; derivation at lowering.
condensed.reason: Already mostly operative; kept all derivation arrows and the unchanged-model invariant, trimmed only minor connective phrasing.

condensed.new_desc: Every component carries a meta slot so configuration is unreachable via the ordinary working-signal surface; if no separate meta-signal repo exists the component repo itself carries the meta-signal surface. Split a contract into separate signal-component and meta-signal-component repos for: rebuild-churn isolation (peers depend on the small contract repo, recompile only on wire-contract changes), security visibility (owner-only ops in a distinct meta repo non-owner clients need not depend on), and optionality (componentless owner relationship needs only the ordinary signal contract). The split is compilation/dependency isolation and authority clarity, not where state or logic lives.
condensed.reason: Kept the meta-slot rule, three split reasons, and the not-about-state caveat; trimmed restatement only.

condensed.new_desc: Components with two authority surfaces use the two-contract triad: two schemas (signal-<c> working/peer-callable, meta-signal-<c> policy/owner-only), each emitting its own Signal/Nexus/SEMA engines and sharing record types; the runtime imports both and runs two listeners on two sockets. lojix ships two CLIs: a meta-socket CLI for meta-signal-lojix (Deploy/Pin/Unpin/Retire, daemon config, owner SO_PEERCRED-auth) and a regular-socket CLI for signal-lojix peer ops (Query/WatchDeployments/WatchCacheRetention/Unwatch). cloud uses signal-cloud (read-only Observe/Validate) and meta-signal-cloud (eight account/plan mutations). cloud and domain components use the current triad signal/sema/executor architecture; future component work uses this latest architecture.
condensed.reason: Kept the two-socket shape, both CLI op sets, the cloud mapping, and the forward-architecture mandate; dropped 'ad hoc shapes' narration.

condensed.new_desc: schema-codegen capability set is closed (types, generic frame expansion, payload structs/newtypes/enums, standard impls, role/marker traits, shape-computed constants, method-bearing trait surface, actor wiring, plane carriers, opt-in Deref). Next phase is integration/migration, not language design: flip scalar-newtype impls default-on, integrate onto code-repo main, and port all components — regenerate spirit, signal-spirit, meta-signal-spirit and the triad through the emitter, swapping generated Rust for hand-wired boilerplate. Pipeline: each plane defined in schema, assembled into Asschema, emitted as Rust datatypes plus basic trait impls; only abstract engine bodies (decide, validate) stay hand-written as the decision-making actor system over schema-emitted nouns. Spirit is a copyable contract-daemon/engine-stack exemplar, not an all-in-one pilot. Runtime binaries stay small carrying only strict rkyv wire/storage contracts; the schema/NOTA compiler (nota-next, schema-next, schema-rust-next) is build-time-only, never linked into runtime, and the NOTA text codec is an optional edge feature absent from the daemon.
condensed.reason: Restored the plane->Asschema->Rust assembly arrow the first pass blurred into 'over schema-emitted nouns'; kept the closed-set inventory, migration steps, exemplar mandate, and build-time-only/no-NOTA-in-daemon boundaries.

condensed.new_desc: The schema-derived engine mechanism is workspace-canonical: NexusWork/NexusAction asymmetric pair + five-variant action set (ReplyToSignal, CommandSemaWrite, CommandSemaRead, CommandEffect, Continue); macro-generated runner loop (triad_main! from schema-rust-next); per-component effects declared in schema (Stash first); Continue as in-process recursion; cross-component invocation via Signal contracts, not Nexus-internal access. These reaction-frame types (Work, Action, five-variant set) are universal — declared once, bound per component, never re-authored. Emitted triad engines ARE kameo actors (minimal on_start/on_stop now; backpressure/scheduling deferred); runtime nouns named Actor must be real Kameo actors. A universal Interact trait — interact(other), async when awaiting outbound queries like criome authorization — formalizes that all logic is interactions between domains.
condensed.reason: Kept all canonical components, the actors-everywhere rule, and the Interact trait; dropped report-citation and 'drift' war-story framing (the affirmative 'engines ARE kameo actors' carries the point).

condensed.new_desc: Router owns a standardized routing protocol: a router-typed envelope carries routing/object metadata for a serialized contract-owned rkyv payload, stays payload-blind beyond envelope/routing/authentication/delivery policy, and is the sole operational subscription matcher for non-direct passing. Object-update fan-out: criome stamps/authorizes the object and emits an authorized-object reference (not payload); Router matches subscriptions and fans references to subscribers who fetch the rkyv object. Accepted objects are quorum-backed by default. Time-driven pulses use an after-time contract scheduling a later check that triggers a new quorum-signed acceptance if its events did not occur. First production milestone is spirit->criome->router->mirror: Spirit asks local criome to authenticate a content-addressed object, criome verifies/signs under a cluster-root identity and propagates via Router, the remote mirror fetches/restores state — criome auth-only, Router transport-only, mirror the version-control substrate. One mirror per node (symmetric with per-system router); receiving mirror owns fetch/restore after the authenticated head notice. The mirror is the psyche's cross-machine self: editing on any owner machine propagates as a criome-authorized state transition; criome holds authority on the latest approved head and Spirit fetches it. The mirror aspect may fold into the Spirit daemon.
condensed.reason: Kept every architectural arrow (envelope/payload-blind, fan-out ownership, quorum default, after-time pulses, the four-component chain, per-node topology, mirror-as-self, possible fold); trimmed only narration phrasing.

condensed.new_desc: Component triad split: client-facing Signal types live in the signal-<component> contract repo (canonical; daemon imports them); Nexus and SEMA interfaces live in the daemon repo as runtime-internal. SEMA may extract to a contract repo when a daemon gains a scale-out database. Cross-schema imports reference another schema's locally-defined namespace types, leaving room for a future shared schema library; per-schema self-definition suffices for MVP. The schema type/macro-substrate library may co-locate in the triad or sit in a dedicated schema-types repo consuming the same compiled NOTA artifact — only crate-layout differs.
condensed.reason: Dropped 'deferred but design leaves room' and 'lean unsettled' hedging narration; kept the repo-placement arrows, SEMA extraction trigger, cross-schema import rule, and library-co-location option.

condensed.new_desc: Schema reader is multi-pass NOTA-first: one universal NOTA parser yields a generic NotaValue tree, context-sensitive macro passes interpret each value per its NodeDefinitionPoint position, then assembly produces canonical schema. Parsing decomposes by object via positional source spans; each block carries shape predicates (bracket/paren/brace kind, root-object count, recursive nesting checks) and each root object is itself a typed positioned block, so recursive parsing is a chain of block-level queries. Delimiter roles: parens=enum/variant, square=vector/token-vector, braces=name-value namespace map. Pass 1 finds identifier uses+locations; pass 2 registers inline declarations and resolves references, supporting bare-PascalCase-or-Variant-Payload shorthand, with source representation deciding define/reference/both. NotaBodyDecode is the semantic entry: document/block decode strip wrapper context and delegate to the body decoder, which reads the inner object stream per the expected type (struct/vector/enum/value). Schema has its own typed source-node model (structural-macro nodes, namespaces, paths, fields, variants, references) before assembly and acts as its own codec so shorthand round-trips. Block reassembly concatenates parsed blocks in order as first-class units.
condensed.reason: Kept all distinct arrows (multi-pass model, block predicates, delimiter roles, two passes, NotaBodyDecode delegation, typed source-node model+codec, reassembly); compressed restatement and 'one syntax/one parser' framing prose.

condensed.new_desc: Local AI on Prometheus: install both big Gemma multimodal models — ~31B dense (max quality) and ~26B MoE (a4b, ~4B active/token, fast) — at bf16 with f16 vision projector, and deploy multiple quantization variants as separate selectable models in the llama-server router, each named by quant in modelId (e.g. gemma-4-26b-a4b-bf16), sharing one mmproj per base model so users pick quality vs speed. Local Pi and agent sessions select these as a local provider. Prefer one curated best/latest model per capability over near-duplicates (prefetch large candidates freely; models need not run simultaneously). Video speech-to-text is tiered: best path on the large-AI node, smaller local STT for weaker machines.
condensed.reason: Already mostly operative; trimmed the memory/storage justification aside, kept all model/quant/router/provider/STT arrows.

condensed.new_desc: Short-header structure: a 64-bit micro-message header (1 root enum + 7 sub-enums) discriminates any namespace object in constant time, emitted by every signal contract macro. Byte 0 is the ROOT VERB (Help/Query/Message); bytes 1-7 are verb-namespace sub-variants. In schema the header is a positional [Variant ...] vector; data-carrying variants (paren name+data) take slots 0-6, unit variants (bare PascalCase) follow, so adding a unit variant is a no-op wire upgrade. Root variants limited, nested ones broad. Sub-enums default to one byte but pack tighter by inner type (Bool=1 bit, Option=2, 16-variant enum=4 bits), letting SEMA beat raw rkyv; freed bits encode deeper nesting and multi-byte sub-enums cover >256 variants within 64 bits. Integers are typed values, not sub-enum kinds. Three sizing tiers: (1) 64-bit micro header, (2) 64-byte summary message (hand-built from natural summary fields, basis for a subscription tier), (3) full unrestricted rkyv message.
condensed.reason: Compressed framing prose; preserved every arrow (byte layout, slot ordering rule, packing tiers, integer rule, three sizing tiers).

condensed.new_desc: Each agent has its own Criome identity created at agent start and recorded in persona-mind; its Criome public key is the authoritative authentication anchor. Complex (especially agent-initiated) Criome interactions run through the agent's own Criome daemon; authorization for many workspace operations checks against Criome, not SSH, and any component/daemon can escalate to Criome for a check. Substrate is per-component-per-domain agent spaces. Criome is per-Unix-user: each user runs their own home Criome daemon with own key custody, the Unix-user boundary being the trust boundary — no shared multi-user system daemon. The privileged system Criome tier serves host-scoped system services under a service user that a home Criome peers with, not a shared daemon; the same single-user custody boundary rules out an embeddable Criome engine library.
condensed.reason: Dropped the 'collapse every user behind one compromise point' justification narration; kept identity anchor, daemon routing, Criome-over-SSH authz, per-user custody boundary, system tier, and no-embeddable-library arrows.

condensed.new_desc: Wire-header pattern grows by extension not replacement: the 8-byte Tier 1 micro-type header is the base and lives as a parseable prefix inside longer (256/512/variable) headers holding most of the frame structure. A header declares an enum identifier plus ordered endpoint nodes and encodes enough top-level variant shape for cheap routing; schema declares which fields fit in 8 bytes vs extended positions, with data vectors following as header-indexed memory arrays. Every message carries the 64-bit micro-type marker (type, size, root structure), rkyv zero-copy after a network-fingerprint check, the prefix doubling as a default-on tap-anywhere observation channel. An extended 512/64-byte Tier 2 sits between the micro variant and the full rkyv record for payloads needing public keys, identities, signatures, or short descriptions (e.g. a Criome quorum authorization payload).
condensed.reason: Trimmed restatement; kept extension-not-replacement principle, header contents, fit-in-8-bytes rule, micro-marker+fingerprint+tap-anywhere, and Tier 2 use case.

condensed.new_desc: Telos = umbrella meta-project (Greek: ultimate end), far horizon eventual Criome + Sema, realized now as Persona (meta-AI of LLMs over typed dumb mechanism). criome is Telos's agreement-and-authorization organ (universal agreement machine for authorization), not Telos. Quorum is criome's universal primitive: one membership-scoped quorum serves both reliability/credible-time (a principal's own multi-node) and cross-party trust (multi-principal). criome's internal language is a limited typed policy language over public-key identity atoms (not a general VM; discipline from Ethereum/Tezos/Solana VMs), composing identity contracts from k-of-n signature quorums and time-locks with time-varying thresholds plus explicit divergence-reconciliation objects (conflict resolution may invoke LLM-oracle). Verdicts come from gathered BLS quorum signatures, a connected approver over criome's meta socket (mentci is that client), or configured auto-approve policy (bootstrap/testing); contracts escalate to a named adjudicator (mechanical quorum, LLM panel, smarter agent, or psyche) whose signed content-addressed verdict criome only verifies. Verdicts are closed typed outcomes (approve/reject/defer/non-judge/escalate) over already-submitted objects, never carry new content; non-judgment is first-class, default LLM judge leans abstain+escalate, psyche is highest-authority lowest-availability (EscalateToPsyche inert dead-letter until psyche UI exists). criome may run as multiple deployment instances (system + home/user), same two contracts differing by privilege tier. domain-criome is the Criome domain component, excludes provider APIs and direct CLI store access. signal-standard is the second shared signal- library (alongside signal-frame): ComponentKind roster+differentiator (closed-but-partitioned), then standard typed connection points (Unix socket path, optional port).
condensed.reason: Every clause is a distinct arrow; restored 'for authorization' on universal agreement machine, 'general VM', and 'dead-letter' qualifier. Referents all named particulars in source. Near-essay but irreducibly dense.

condensed.new_desc: Type end-to-end, never string-keyed: extend the typed model first (e.g. add a real NodeService enum variant with NotaEncode/Decode in the horizon model), author the fact, project typed, consume typed. Typed-record vocabulary = trees of branches (variable-size/data-carrying: vectors, data-carrying enum variants, structs with variable members) and leaves (fixed-size terminals like unit-variant enums). SymbolPath is a flat vector of Name segments recovering role from schema position. A pure-projection library (Horizon, no runtime triad) needs a types-only-module schema variant so datatypes generate without forcing an Input/Output signal plane. SignalCore holds workspace-universal core types in the 64-bit namespace partition; each component contributes its own zone.
condensed.reason: Stripped wrong-behavior narration (string keys/or-empty-list/fixtures bypass) into affirmative typed-pipeline rule; kept vocabulary, SymbolPath, types-only variant, SignalCore arrows. Dropped unreferenced 'criomos' from original referents (no arrow uses it); added nota (NotaEncode/Decode).

condensed.new_desc: Horizon splits into RAW horizon (core typed cluster-data model: NodeService enum, typed facts, arch resolution, name/key-line newtypes, cross-node fan-in, typed validation, secret-binding resolution) and a separate PRETTY horizon layer that computes pre-derived variables in typed Rust (not Nix): behaves-as/gating booleans, resolved magnitudes, lid-switch policy, trust-gated extra-groups; Nix consumes only already-derived variables. Split is also a forward-integration seam: raw stays clean+minimal to later promote into a real component, pretty stays the typed derive layer. Applies ONLY to the new lojix/horizon-next stack; production Stack A horizon stays as-is until cutover. Spend the cleanliness budget on the durable layer that outlives the bootstrap substrate (Nix, replaced by future Forge); unavoidable glue ugliness lives in the throwaway substrate.
condensed.reason: Condensed rationale prose, kept raw/pretty split, Nix-consumes-derived, forward-integration seam, lojix-only scope, Forge-replaces-Nix arrows. Added named referents horizon-next, node-service, stack-a.

condensed.new_desc: LojixOS split is a rename-and-configuration split: generic OS substrate becomes LojixOS; CriomOS becomes a simple per-deploy configuration crate holding a thin NOTA data tree that lojix precompiles into binary config. Cloud-node OS images live in CriomOS as a minimal CloudNode-species profile, declaratively built with an image builder (not by snapshotting a converted running droplet) and content-sized to boot fast on the smallest sensible droplet (multi-tens-of-GB unacceptable). Per-node configuration is declared in the cluster data (horizon cluster proposal). Cloud daemon references the image by id via HostPlan image_name and never holds the image definition; CriomOS owns the image definition + provider-format build, cloud owns snapshot-id selection and provisioning.
condensed.reason: Trimmed connectives, kept rename split, image-builder-not-snapshot, size limit, ownership boundary arrows. Restored 'lojix' alongside 'lojixos' (both named in source: lojix precompiles); added host-plan, nota.

condensed.new_desc: lojix-daemon owns GitHub-authenticated Nix flake input resolution (the GitHub API rate-limit stale-activation incident is a deploy-path problem owned by lojix, not a Pi-package problem; resolves system-designer/31 OQ 7.2 toward lojix-daemon scope over skill-level discipline). Mechanism: an authenticated wrapper around the Nix invocation fetching the user GitHub API key from gopass and injecting it into the Nix call (likely via NIX_CONFIG access-tokens). Form: a small Rust library lojix-daemon calls that encapsulates secret-fetch + auth-injection so the rest of lojix-daemon never handles the token directly.
condensed.reason: Compressed incident war-story and report citations to pointers; kept ownership decision, gopass+NIX_CONFIG mechanism, and encapsulating-library form. Added nix-config referent.

condensed.new_desc: criome's clock is decentralized quorum-attested coarse crystallized-PAST time (no single trusted/precise clock; only the past is provable). A time attestation begins as a forward-extending window proposition (content-addressed as its identity); a quorum signs that the present falls within it, closing at last signature or expiry, crystallizing a non-forgeable monotonic lower bound on now. Window extends forward so every member can sign; tolerance gap bounded below by quorum signing latency, widening under partition. Every quorum-signed object carries an AttestedMoment (a signature is never timeless), so every admission envelope, verdict, agreement fact, and triad input/output is time-bound for freshness/replay; time-locks compare against the operation's own proof-of-when. AttestedMoment is the self-grounding base case carrying no inner moment, stopping the regress.
condensed.reason: Light trim of connectives, preserved all clock-design arrows: window proposition, content-addressed identity, lower-bound crystallization, latency floor, per-object moment, regress base case. Referents unchanged (all named particulars).

condensed.new_desc: Testing and deployment are one function in the lojix deploy component: both build an OS/cluster closure and bring it up on a target, differing only in containment. Triad exposes both faces — ordinary non-meta signal targets contained throwaway resources (hermetic VMs, sandboxes, on-demand VmHost guests, ephemeral cloud droplets it provisions and reaps) so a broken run kills only the contained target; meta signal is privileged production deploy to real nodes. The ordinary-vs-meta split is the safety boundary, enforced by typed contained-vs-production targets, not a runtime flag.
condensed.reason: Stripped the supersedes-report-156 war-story; kept the unify-deploy-and-test arrow, the two-face/containment split, the kills-only-contained-target rationale, and the typed-target safety boundary. Dropped orphaned cpip referent (its war-story clause was removed).

condensed.new_desc: Authored-schema declaration forms: omit only the root object name (reader knows root type); every nested object names itself. Enum = (qualified-name, paren-of-qualified-symbols), must name its type (bare variant list invalid); struct = bracket second-object form; one-symbol bracket = newtype. Root struct fields are bare; Name@Delimiter applies only to user declarations of new type names — additional plane roots (NexusInput/Output, SemaInput/Output) keep Name@. Inline PascalCase before references = local reusable types; camelCase = field names. Pipe = generics/traits; assembled schema stays legal NOTA. Spirit schema files: no outer root parens, header/root first, enums as ordered variant vectors, cover ordinary/owner/Sema headers.
condensed.reason: Tightened phrasing and added sema/nexus referents. Restored the 'assembled schema stays legal NOTA' arrow and the 'bare variant list invalid' clause that the first pass blurred; preserved every other declaration-form clause.

condensed.new_desc: Resolve the enum-vs-macro-invocation ambiguity on paren heads with an explicit discriminator: a collection macro (e.g. Vec on one element type) and a two-variant enum are structurally identical parens at the same node position, so delimiter-shape/position cannot distinguish operator-head from variant-name. Only the paren is doubly loaded; struct bracket and map brace are unambiguous. Candidate discriminators: reserved operator-word set (implicit), a sigil on operator heads (explicit), or moving collections off the paren onto suffix sigils + brace (vector=star suffix, option=question suffix, map=brace) leaving paren pure enum. Open: which discriminator, and whether enum stays the bare-paren default.
condensed.reason: Removed the 'corrects earlier framing that position alone resolves it' narration; kept the requirement for an explicit discriminator, the three candidates, and the open decision. Referent vec is present in desc.

condensed.new_desc: Component code-generation (designed/prototyped, not integrated): schema generates a component's Rust by expanding bound universal frames into concrete owned interfaces, not persisting generic aliases. Scope is narrow — repeated interface boilerplate: Input/Output and work/action enums, payload structs/newtypes/enums, standard inherent/trait impls (constructors, accessors, From legs, scalar-newtype Display/AsRef/comparisons), role/marker trait impls, opt-in mechanical Deref for the newtype shape, later VariantMatch for enum-rewrap. Traits/impls are the pipe-brace construct expressing Rust relationships as data so hand-written engine code accepts generated nouns via typed bounds. Method bodies are data only for the small mechanical family the schema shape uniquely proves (newtype Deref projection); business logic stays hand-written. Principle: a schema-authored body is valid only when every call resolves from the receiver's schema shape. Open: generic impl-header emission with parameter-bound vocabulary, fn/signature sub-construct for trait declarations, ownership-aware payload-vs-into_payload projection, total VariantMatch; settled constraints and open how live in schema-next/nota-next architecture files.
condensed.reason: Trimmed redundant framing; kept the expand-not-alias direction, the boilerplate scope list, the pipe-brace traits-as-data, the schema-shape-resolves-every-call validity principle, and the open pieces. Added referents present in desc.
